domain: Norwegian fjord 3-layer stratification (brackish/coastal/Atlantic) with pycnocline density-gradient interface limiting vertical energy transfer
specific_mechanism: Sharp density-gradient interface (pycnocline) passively dissipates kinetic energy at layer boundaries; freshwater stays on top; deep water turnover is slow; no active gating.
llm_application: PYCNO-DAMP: install a passive 'density-mismatch' parameter (a learned per-block scaling that creates large differential between layer-group output statistics) at K specific inter-layer interfaces in a transformer; the mismatch passively dissipates back-propagated gradients from upper layers before they reach lower layers, preventing high-amplitude gradient bursts from disrupting frozen early-layer features. Combined with sill-style hard-stop: deepest layers receive at most epsilon gradient flow regardless of upper-layer perturbation.
novelty_claim: Pre-norm/Peri-LN normalize at sub-layer boundaries to stabilize gradients (smooth, uniform). Spike No More shrinks embedding gradients via residual scaling. DS-Init / DeepNet scale parameter variance by depth. PYCNO-DAMP differs by installing DELIBERATE INTERFACIAL DENSITY-MISMATCH between layer groups (3-zone fjord-style stratification) where each zone has independent gradient dynamics and the inter-zone barriers are passive dissipators — not smooth/uniform stabilization but explicit zoning of gradient propagation regimes.
